author: @joedee1863
text: OK it was a good laugh but it reminds me of a contraption I saw in a park in the fifties. A device that converted the energy from 2 people stepping onto a tread board. They were facing each other and taking turns to step on and off. A spring was partly returning the year board for the other guy to depress but a fly wheel was also involved to keep up the momentum and because the tread frame was connected and pivoted one man stepping on returned the tread plate for the other guy. This was on a track and propelled the whole thing around a track. The interesting thing to me was it took such little effort to step on and off compared to pedalling which is wear and tear on joints and ligaments. Even kids and the elderly were having fun with it. I never seen anything like it since and when I have spoken to engineers about it they try to convince me a thing like that couldn't work ! So what I saw in the fifties must have been a hallucination 😁

author: @duncyy7471
text: hi, I dont know if you already have an idea for the generator part but take a look at those hoverboard or oxboard things. You can get a broken one for 10/15 euro/usd and it uses of 2x 350watt brushless bldc motors. Ad some rectifiers to the output wires and you have a dc generator. Maybe you could also change the wires inside from wye to delta (really easy) for a lower voltage, higher current output. i don't know what is the best/most efficient setup.

author: @AvilerED
text: I enjoy the joy you find in these simple ideas. Personally, I have a bit darker reason for being interested in this. We live in a very fragile balance with technology that can potentially be catastrophically upset. Knowing all these simple ways to do things, whether it's these wooden gears, your peg gears, your various passive power generation ideas (I know, not your ideas, per se, but ideas you've found and shared and demonstrated here on YT) could be the difference between simply surviving and potentially flourishing, in my perception. Cheers, and thank you!

author: @vylbird8014
text: Not fast enough - the output gear is far too slow to drive most easily-available alternators. Suggest you give up wood for the final stage, and mount a pully on the same shaft as the output that you can then couple to a smaller pulley on the alternator - that design would let you use either a DC motor, or an alternator repurposed from a car. If the latter, I have a circuit which replaces a car alternator voltage regulator module to allow you more control over it, including the option of regulating to any output voltage you want.

author: @SkippedTilly
text:
Watching your cuboctahedron vids again... I wonder how well it could capture energy from another turbulent fluid medium like the eddy currents produced by large rocks and sharp bends in a moving stream of water... would be an interesting concept to float one into a fast moving stream. I live in the central part of the u.s.a. where lots of people own property where a moving water source runs thru their property, would be a neat concept for anyone capturing enough turbulent water to take advantage of hydroelectric power without the need for a dam that might cause issues with the government entities that always want to stifle innovation. 

Just a wayward thought. ❤

author: @Buzzhumma
text: I can imagine a solid 100 kg weight being elevated with this contraption to another level and producing power through a gear made of same but i have always liked the 10:1 leverage of human weight on a seesaw. You use your bodyweight to raise say 800 kg about 300 mm from ground which takes very little energy and you recoup a huge amount of energy in return via various apparatus ! As long as your alive you have unlimited power!

replies.text: for sure mate

replies.author: @Buzzhumma
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering using the leverage to lift your car up 100mm would be easy but how to harness the power on the way down . Gets me thinking about you bellow air conditioner!!!

author: @sean-or1nc
text: Did  you know to use gorilla glue properly you have to wet a side to be glued? The moisture reacts with the glue and expands it and makes it a stronger bond?  Took me a while to realize this because who reads the directions label ..

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: yep - but I live in England - we have plenty of moist air -  but you are right and it makes a better bond - thanks for tip mate

author: @Cooliemasteroz
text: Very well done. While I understand why you didn’t use it to make electricity it actually would’ve been a good thing to show people just how hard it is to power an electric jug and try to boil some water. This could then make people appreciate just how much power we use every day and not take it for granted and hopefully stop people from wasting it. I imagine that most of your viewers do not waste electricity but they could show such a demonstration to others.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: nice idea mate - cheers

author: @gameoverwehaveeverypixelco1258
text:
Bit of a weird coincidence, I was watching a tv infomercial that was selling a hand crank powered portable washing machine and I thought I wonder if you could attach some foot pedals to that and power it. I was thinking more like the long foot pedals that the old wool spindles that create wool yarn, the type from sleeping beauty.
You'd get more spin too just by pushing down on the long pedal.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I like that mate - the victorians did some awesome stuff with pedal power

author: @buffplums
text: Hey Robert this is great love the MDF cogs ... hey why don’t you get an old car alternator, all you need is an initial 12V DC to initially power the field coils and you could easily do that with a small SLA battery and that could be a simple cheap solution to keep it all working. You will get a good output from an alternator too.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: nice one mate- will do

author: @michaeltucker8645
text: Rob this is what I used to turn my car alternator generator.  Bit smaller gears but same principle. I took a wood gear cut it in half cut out the center first to fit around the pulley gear on the alternator  and used some epoxy putty to fit it on the alternator with some dowels and wood glue and the other I turn with 3 pack of 775 motors the drive train is aslo all wood .  I suggest an alternator  they are cheap at pull a part if your old parts car doesnt have one

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: nice one ate - I'll do just that - cheers

author: @stevetobias4890
text:
Bloody excellent. Good that you don't attach a small stepper motor to attract the criticism revolving around the fact you don't have the right DC motors the project requires. People need to remember that your about the concept of ideas and the realm of possibilities not creating saleable finished products.

Great video, love the enthusiastic laugh after pedaling as with other workable projects.
